name: finapi
description: Search finAPI's bank catalog, inspect client configuration, and provision end users for BaFin-regulated open banking access

envVars.name: FINAPI_CLIENT_ID
envVars.description: finAPI Access client identifier issued with your license or sandbox registration

envVars.name: FINAPI_CLIENT_SECRET
envVars.description: finAPI Access client secret paired with the client identifier

tools.name: Search Banks
tools.description: Search finAPI's database of supported banks by name, BLZ, BIC, or city, with interface and location filters

search.description: Only banks whose name, BLZ, BIC, or city matches this search term

ids.description: Comma-separated list of bank identifiers to fetch

location.description: Comma-separated two-letter ISO 3166-1 country codes, e.g. DE,AT

supportedBankingInterfaces.description: Comma-separated banking interfaces to require: FINTS_SERVER, WEB_SCRAPER, XS2A

isTestBank.description: Filter to test banks (true) or real banks (false)

order.description: Order results by id, name, blz, bic, or popularity, e.g. name,asc

perPage.description: Maximum number of records per page (max 500)

historicalSummary.omitted: interface capability details, TPP authentication groups, and login hint texts

tools.name: Get Bank
tools.description: Retrieve a single bank from finAPI's catalog by its identifier, including supported interfaces and login requirements

tools.name: Get Client Configuration
tools.description: Get your finAPI client's configuration: token validity periods, callback URLs, enabled bank sets, and consent settings

tools.name: Create User
tools.description: Provision a new finAPI end user (one per application end user); user-level operations then authenticate with that user's own token

id.description: User identifier (max 36 chars, letters, digits and -). Auto-generated if omitted

password.description: User password (min 6, max 36 chars). A unique random value is generated if omitted

isAutoUpdateEnabled.description: Whether the user's bank connections get updated by finAPI's automatic batch update

prompts.title: Find a bank
prompts.prompt: Search finAPI for a German bank by name or BLZ and show which banking interfaces it supports.

prompts.title: Client configuration
prompts.prompt: Show my finAPI client configuration and summarize token validity periods and enabled bank sets.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: finAPI setup

steps.title: Get client credentials
steps.description: Request finAPI Access credentials (client_id and client_secret) from finAPI at https://www.finapi.io/en/contact-us/. New customers start with a free sandbox client; the Live environment requires a license agreement.

steps.title: Set environment variables
steps.description: Add FINAPI_CLIENT_ID and FINAPI_CLIENT_SECRET to your .env.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run Get Client Configuration. Access tokens are minted automatically via the OAuth2 client_credentials grant against https://live.finapi.io/api/v2/oauth/token — no user login is involved.

notes: This connector pins the Live environment (live.finapi.io). For sandbox testing, point a copy of the connector at sandbox.finapi.io — the token and API paths are identical. Live access may additionally be IP-restricted.

Most finAPI data endpoints (bank connections, accounts, transactions, securities) require a USER access token obtained per end user via the password grant. The client_credentials token used here only covers client-level endpoints: bank catalog search, client configuration, and user provisioning.
Users created on the Live instance count towards your finAPI license agreement.
